title: AI Engineer Interview Questions
description: Interview question bank converted from the AI Engineer interview curriculum for bot integration.

sections.title: The Most Important Insight for Your Preparation
Tier 1: Agents, Tool Calling, and Workflows
Tier 1: Prompt Engineering and Structured Outputs
Tier 1: LLM Evaluation, Observability, and Reliability
Tier 2: MLOps, Cloud, and Data Engineering

What to Prioritize Given Your Background
A Portfolio Project That Covers Most Questions
Search Terms Worth Monitoring on LinkedIn

questions.text: How would you structure a production FastAPI service for an LLM-powered application?
When should an endpoint be synchronous, asynchronous, or delegated to a background worker?
What happens internally when Python code uses `async` and `await`?
Why can an asynchronous API still become slow or blocked?
How would you implement request timeouts, retries, and exponential backoff for an external model API?
Which failures should be retried, and which failures should immediately return an error?
What is idempotency, and why is it important when an AI agent calls external tools?
How would you stream generated tokens to a frontend?
When would you use Server-Sent Events rather than WebSockets?
How would you validate a model-generated JSON response?
What should happen when an LLM returns malformed structured output?
How would you rate-limit an AI API?
How would you implement caching for repeated LLM requests?
Which parts of an AI pipeline are safe to cache?
How would you protect secrets such as model-provider API keys?
How would you test an API that depends on a probabilistic model?
How would you mock an LLM provider during automated testing?
What information should be logged for each AI request?
What information should never be written into logs?
How would you trace a request across an API, retriever, model call, and external tool?
What problem does the Transformer architecture solve?
Explain self-attention in practical terms.
What is a token, and why does tokenization affect cost and latency?
What is a context window?
What happens when a prompt exceeds the context window?
What is the difference between an encoder-only, decoder-only, and encoder-decoder model?
When would you use BERT-like models rather than a generative LLM?
What is the difference between an embedding model and a generative model?
What do temperature, top-p, and maximum token limits control?
Why can lowering temperature improve consistency but not guarantee correctness?
What is hallucination?
Why do hallucinations occur?
How do hosted proprietary models differ from self-hosted open-weight models?
What factors determine whether a model should be self-hosted?
How would you select a model for a production feature?
How would you compare two LLMs fairly?
How do model size, quality, latency, memory usage, and cost interact?
What is a multimodal model?
What is a Vision-Language Model?
What is a reasoning model, and when is it worth the additional latency?
What is Retrieval-Augmented Generation?
Which problems does RAG solve?
When should you use RAG instead of fine-tuning?
When should you combine RAG and fine-tuning?
Describe a complete RAG pipeline from raw documents to generated answer.
What happens during document ingestion?
What is chunking?
How would you choose an appropriate chunk size?
What is chunk overlap, and what trade-offs does it introduce?
Why can naive fixed-size chunking reduce answer quality?
When would semantic chunking be useful?
Which metadata should be stored alongside document chunks?
What is an embedding?
How would you select an embedding model?
What is cosine similarity?
When might dot product or Euclidean distance be used instead?
What is semantic search?
What is keyword search?
What is BM25?
What is hybrid search?
Why can hybrid retrieval outperform pure vector search?
What is reranking?
Why is a cross-encoder reranker often more accurate but slower than embedding similarity?
How many documents should be retrieved before reranking?
How many documents should be passed into the final prompt?
How would you handle duplicate or near-duplicate retrieved chunks?
How would you implement metadata filtering?
How would you isolate data belonging to different customers?
How would you enforce document-level access control?
How would you update an index when documents change?
How would you delete outdated documents reliably?
How would you process PDFs, tables, images, and scanned documents?
How would you retrieve information from both structured SQL data and unstructured text?
What is query rewriting?
What is multi-query retrieval?
What is multi-hop retrieval?
What is GraphRAG?
When does a knowledge graph provide value over a conventional vector store?
What is agentic RAG?
What is the difference between retrieval failure and generation failure?
What capabilities should a vector database provide?
What is an approximate nearest-neighbor index?
Why are approximate search algorithms used instead of exact search at scale?
What are the practical differences between FAISS, pgvector, Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus, and Chroma?
When is PostgreSQL with pgvector sufficient?
When would you introduce a dedicated vector database?
What are HNSW and IVF indexes?
How would you tune retrieval latency versus recall?
How would you monitor the health of a vector-search system?
How would you migrate embeddings after changing the embedding model?
How would you evaluate retrieval quality separately from answer quality?
What are Recall@K, Precision@K, Mean Reciprocal Rank, and NDCG?
How would you create a RAG evaluation dataset?
What is groundedness?
What is faithfulness?
How would you detect unsupported claims in a generated answer?
How would you debug a RAG system that returns fluent but incorrect answers?
How would you determine whether the problem lies in ingestion, chunking, embeddings, retrieval, reranking, or generation?
How would you measure the effect of adding a reranker?
How would you prevent stale information from being returned?
What is an AI agent?

questions.text: Design an internal RAG assistant for a company with 500,000 documents and role-based document access.
Design a customer-support copilot that searches documentation, retrieves CRM data, and drafts an answer for human approval.
Design an agent that updates a CRM after a sales call without creating duplicate records.
Design a document-intelligence pipeline that processes PDFs, tables, images, and scanned documents.
Design an AI assistant that must run on-premises because customer data cannot leave the company network.
Design an LLM gateway that routes easy tasks to a cheap model and difficult tasks to a stronger model.
Design an evaluation pipeline for a RAG assistant before and after each deployment.
Design an observability dashboard for an agentic workflow.
Design a fallback strategy for a model-provider outage.
Design a safe SQL assistant for business analysts.
Design a GraphRAG system for a domain with relationships between people, companies, contracts, and events.
Design a multimodal assistant that analyzes text, images, and audio.
